message:
fix(storage): rewrite find_cycles to deduplicate and bound traversal depth

The previous iterative DFS-with-rec-stack implementation of
SqliteStorage::find_cycles had two real problems on messy dependency
graphs:

  1. It could emit the same logical cycle multiple times under different
     rotations (e.g. A->B->C->A and B->C->A->B) because it reported
     whatever rotation the outer DFS start-node happened to produce. The
     de-duplication downstream assumed canonical rotations and didn't
     get them.
  2. It had no depth cap. On dense or corrupted graphs the search could
     blow up exponentially before finally returning.

New implementation:

  * Canonicalize per cycle: for each candidate start_node, only record a
    cycle if start_node is the strict minimum node in that cycle
    (enforced by the `v > &start_node` gate on the recursion + the
    explicit "closes only back to start_node" check). Any other
    rotation of the same cycle will be discovered when its smaller
    member becomes start_node, so the HashSet<Vec<String>> sees a
    single canonical entry per cycle.

  * Path-carrying stack: the DFS stack now carries (node, path) pairs
    so cycle reconstruction is trivial — when we see `v == start_node`
    we push path.clone() + [start_node] straight into unique_cycles.
    No more separate rec_stack/path/visited bookkeeping.

  * Depth cap: `max_depth = DEPENDENCY_TRAVERSAL_MAX_DEPTH.min(20)`
    prevents pathological fan-out. 20 is large enough for any realistic
    project-level dependency chain while keeping the worst case bounded.

  * Deterministic output: collect into a Vec and .sort() before
    returning, so callers (and tests) see a stable ordering independent
    of HashSet iteration order.

Net effect: `br` cycle reports no longer list the same cycle N times
with different starting points, and `find_cycles` is safe to run on
accidentally-dense graphs.
